title: Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) CEO Jim Umpleby on Q1 2022 Results - Earnings Call Transcript
text: Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT ) Q1 2022 Earnings Conference Call April 28, 2022 8:30 AM ET Company Participants Ryan Fiedler - Director, Investor Relations Jim Umpleby - Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Andrew Bonfield - Chief Financial Officer Kyle Epley - Vice President, Global Finance Services Rob Rengel - Senior Investor Relations Manager Conference Call Participants Rob Wertheimer - Melius Research Jamie Cook - Credit Suisse Mig Dobre - Baird Steve Volkmann - Jefferies David Raso - Evercore Tami Zakaria - JPMorgan Chad Dillard - Bernstein Nicole DeBlase - Deutsche Bank Ross Gilardi - Bank of America Courtney Yakavonis - Morgan Stanley Seth Weber - Wells Fargo Steven Fisher - UBS Tim Thein - Citigroup Matt Elkott - Cowen John Joyner - BMO Jerry Revich - Goldman Sachs Operator Welcome to the First Quarter 2022 Caterpillar Earnings Conference Call. Please be advised that today's conference is being recorded.

title: Caterpillar sales surge on strong construction equipment demand, increased dealer inventories
text: The construction machinery and equipment company reported an adjusted quarterly profit of $2.88 per share on revenue of $13.59 billion

title: Q1 GDP Negative; Q1 Earnings Beats from CAT, LLY, MCD
text: GDP reported the first negative quarter since Q2 2020, which was considered the "heart" of the pandemic.

title: Caterpillar (CAT) Q1 Earnings Beat Estimates on Strong Demand
text: Caterpillar's (CAT) first-quarter 2022 results gain on strong demand across all of its segments and higher pricing that helped offset the impact of inflated costs.

title: Why Caterpillar Shares Are Trading Lower Today
text: Caterpillar Inc (NYSE: CAT) reported first-quarter FY22 sales and revenue growth of 14.3% year-over-year to $13.59 billion, beating the consensus of $13.40 billion. Revenue growth was driven by higher sales volume and favorable price realization, partially offset by unfavorable currency.

title: Caterpillar first-quarter sales rise 14% on demand for heavy equipment
text: Caterpillar Inc reported a 14.3% rise in first-quarter revenue on Thursday, as higher construction activity and a rise in commodity prices boosted sales of its heavy equipment across industries.

title: Caterpillar Stock Rises After Earnings Top Wall Street Estimates
text: Caterpillar reports earnings per share of $2.88 from $13.6 billion in sales. Analysts were looking for profit of $2.60 on sales of about $13.3 billion.

title: Caterpillar shares up 1.4% premarket after earnings beat estimates
text: Caterpillar Inc. shares CAT, +1.74% rose 1.4% in premarket trade Thursday, after the maker of construction and mining equipment blew past estimates for the first quarter. Caterpillar posted net income of $1.537 billion, or $2.86 a share, for the quarter, up from $1.530 billion, or $2.77 a share, in the year-earlier period.

title: Caterpillar Reports First-Quarter 2022 Results
text: First-quarter 2022 sales and revenues increased 14% to $13.6 billion First-quarter 2022 profit per share of $2.86; adjusted profit per share of $2.88 Returned $1.4 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends in the quarter DEERFIELD, Ill., April 28, 2022 /PRNewswire/ --  First Quarter ($ in billions except profit per share) 2022 2021 Sales and Revenues $13.6 $11.9 Profit Per Share $2.86 $2.77 Adjusted Profit Per Share $2.88 $2.87 Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE: CAT) announced first-quarter 2022 sales and revenues of $13.6 billion, a 14% increase compared with $11.9 billion in the first quarter of 2021.

title: Caterpillar sales seen lifted by oil, commodity prices
text: Caterpillar Inc is expected to report higher sales in its energy and mining businesses when it reports results on Thursday, aided by rising commodity prices sparked by the war in Ukraine, which could add to the large-equipment maker's reputation as an inflation hedge.

title: Earnings Previews: Caterpillar, Peabody Energy, Southwest Airlines
text: Before markets opened on Tuesday, United Parcel Service beat analysts' profit and revenue estimates for the March quarter.

title: CAT: Growing Backlog And Rising Commodity Prices Suggest Further Upside
text: Commodity prices have lifted off in 2022, with the Bloomberg Commodity Index up 30.11% YTD. Caterpillar's product offerings, which directly support the mining and exploration of these various commodities, should see increased demand as a result of higher prices.
